report_name: Vietnam's OceanLotus APT Pivots to Domestic Spying, Hits Construction and Finance Sectors

threat_actors.name: OceanLotus
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated threat actor aligned with Vietnamese state interests that has pivoted its operations to focus on domestic targets, including a construction firm and stock market investors. The campaigns are noted to potentially align with Vietnam's national anti-corruption efforts.
threat_actors.goals: Domestic espionage and data exfiltration from construction and financial sectors in Vietnam.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Suspected to have been used for initial access against the construction firm's public-facing Microsoft SQL server via an RCE vulnerability.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The update mechanism of the FireAnt MetaKit stock investment application was compromised to deliver a malicious update to a select group of users.

attack_patterns.name: DLL Side-Loading
attack_patterns.description: The SPECTRALVIPER backdoor was loaded by a legitimate, signed executable to make its execution difficult for security tools to detect.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The SPECTRALVIPER backdoor communicated with its C2 server using a domain name chosen to blend in with legitimate financial data traffic. The compromised software update also used unencrypted HTTP.

malware.name: SPECTRALVIPER
malware.description: A custom backdoor used by OceanLotus in domestic espionage campaigns. It was delivered via a supply-chain attack on FireAnt MetaKit and used for data exfiltration. It communicates with C2 over a domain designed to blend in with financial traffic.

vulnerabilities.name: Microsoft SQL Server RCE Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A remote code execution vulnerability in a public-facing Microsoft SQL server was the suspected initial access vector for the intrusion into the Vietnamese construction firm.

campaigns.name: Construction Firm Espionage Campaign
campaigns.description: A year-long espionage campaign by OceanLotus against a major Vietnamese construction corporation, maintaining persistent access and likely gaining entry via an exploited SQL server.
campaigns.objective: Espionage and data exfiltration from a Vietnamese construction firm.

campaigns.name: FireAnt MetaKit Supply-Chain Attack
campaigns.description: A sophisticated supply-chain attack where OceanLotus compromised the update mechanism of the FireAnt MetaKit stock investment application to selectively deploy the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor to stock market investors.
campaigns.objective: Targeted deployment of SPECTRALVIPER to Vietnamese stock market investors for espionage.

identities.name: Vietnamese construction corporation
identities.description: A major Vietnamese infrastructure and transport construction company targeted by OceanLotus in a year-long espionage campaign.

identities.name: Vietnamese stock market investors
identities.description: A select group of stock market investors in Vietnam targeted by OceanLotus through a supply-chain attack on the FireAnt MetaKit software.

identities.name: FireAnt MetaKit
identities.description: A popular stock investment application whose update mechanism was compromised by OceanLotus in a supply-chain attack.

infrastructure.name: SPECTRALVIPER C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure used by the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor, using the domain financemachinelearning.com.

infrastructure.name: FireAnt MetaKit Update Server
infrastructure.description: The legitimate update server for the FireAnt MetaKit software, which was compromised by OceanLotus to deliver malicious updates.

indicators.name: SPECTRALVIPER C2 Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: SPECTRALVIPER C2 DNS Query
id: 7c8a0b3d-1e2f-4a5b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to the known SPECTRALVIPER C2 domain financemachinelearning.com.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/oceanlotus-hits-vietnam-investors-with.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1071.004
  - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'financemachinelearning.com'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - None known
level: high
indicators.description: Detects network connections to the command-and-control domain used by the SPECTRALVIPER backdoor.

indicators.name: Unsigned DLL Loaded by FireAnt.exe
indicators.pattern:
title: Unsigned DLL Loaded by FireAnt.exe
id: 8d9b1c4e-2f3a-4b6c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6e
status: experimental
description: Detects when FireAnt.exe loads an unsigned DLL, a potential indicator of DLL side-loading as seen in the OceanLotus campaign.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/oceanlotus-hits-vietnam-investors-with.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.defense_evasion
logsource:
  category: image_load
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\FireAnt.exe'
    Signed: false
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate but unsigned DLLs used by the application.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects potential DLL side-loading activity associated with the FireAnt MetaKit compromise, based on monitoring for the legitimate application loading unsigned DLLs.

indicators.name: MS SQL Server Spawning Command Shell
indicators.pattern:
title: MS SQL Server Spawning Command Shell
id: 9e0c2d5f-3a4b-4c7d-0e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
status: experimental
description: Detects the Microsoft SQL Server process (sqlservr.exe) spawning a command shell (cmd.exe or powershell.exe), which may indicate exploitation for remote code execution.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/oceanlotus-hits-vietnam-investors-with.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-11
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\sqlservr.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects behavior consistent with the exploitation of a public-facing SQL server, where the database process spawns a command shell.

relationships.source_name: MS SQL Server Spawning Command Shell
